footer.tagline: A curated collection of developer utilities for security, text processing, and everyday workflows — all in one place.
footer.resourcesBody: Found a bug or have an idea for a new tool? <line>Reach out via your preferred channel or open an issue in the project repository.</line>

home.privacyBadge: 100% Client-Side • No Data Leaves Your Browser
home.subheadline: Secure password generators, JSON formatters, encoders, and 30+ developer utilities. Everything runs locally in your browser—your data never leaves your device.
home.privacyFirstBody: All tools run entirely in your browser using JavaScript. No data is ever sent to any server. No analytics, no tracking, no cookies. Your passwords, tokens, and sensitive data stay on your device.

why.instantResultsDesc: No loading, no waiting. Results appear as you type.

home.ctaSubtitle: Explore our full collection of developer tools.

toolsPage.title: Developer Tools
toolsPage.description: {count} client-side tools for security, encoding, generation, and more. All tools run locally in your browser with no data transmission.
toolsPage.searchPlaceholder: Search tools by name or keyword...
toolsPage.noResultsBody: Try adjusting your search or filter criteria

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.subtitle: Learn about our privacy practices, how the tools work, and why you can trust them.
faqPage.privacyBody: Every tool on Dev ToolStack processes your data locally in your browser. No server calls, no analytics, no tracking. Your data stays on your device.

dataSafety.question: Is my data safe using these tools?
dataSafety.answer: Yes. All tools run 100% client-side in your browser. No data is transmitted to any server. Your passwords, tokens, and sensitive information never leave your device. You can verify this by checking your browser’s Network tab—there should be no requests triggered by using a tool.

clientSideExecution.question: How does client-side execution work?
clientSideExecution.answer: When you load a page, the JavaScript for that tool is downloaded to your browser. After that, all processing happens locally on your computer. For example, password generation uses crypto.getRandomValues(), a cryptographically secure random API built into modern browsers. No server is involved.

randomNumbers.question: Are the random numbers truly random?
randomNumbers.answer: We use crypto.getRandomValues(), a cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator (CSPRNG). It sources entropy from your operating system’s randomness pool. This is the same class of API used for generating cryptographic keys in many real-world applications.

offlineUsage.question: Can I use these tools offline?
offlineUsage.answer: Yes. Once a page is loaded, you can disconnect and keep using the tool because the logic runs in your browser. The only limitation is navigation to pages you haven’t loaded yet (unless you cache them).

analyticsTracking.question: Do you use any analytics or tracking?
analyticsTracking.answer: No. We don’t include analytics scripts or tracking pixels. We also avoid collecting IP addresses or usage data. Privacy tools should practice what they preach.

trust.question: Why should I trust these tools?
trust.answer: Three reasons: (1) Everything runs locally—verify via Network tab. (2) We rely on well-established, browser-native APIs like crypto.getRandomValues(). (3) The security approach is transparent and follows common best practices (e.g., avoiding modulo bias in random generation).

productionUse.question: Are these tools suitable for production use?
productionUse.answer: For many use cases, yes—passwords/tokens/UUIDs are suitable. For operations like bcrypt hashing, we recommend doing it server-side in production. The bcrypt tool is best treated as educational/demo unless you use a real bcrypt library and understand the constraints.

browserSupport.question: What browsers are supported?
browserSupport.answer: All modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest versions). The tools require JavaScript and crypto.getRandomValues(). Internet Explorer is not supported.

contributeBugs.question: Can I contribute or report bugs?
contributeBugs.answer: Yes—feedback is welcome. If you find a bug or have a feature request, share it with us. Security-related issues are especially appreciated.

whyCreateSite.question: Why create yet another developer tools site?
whyCreateSite.answer: Many online tools process data on servers (and often include analytics). We wanted a truly private, fast, client-side toolbox where developers can work with sensitive data without transmission concerns.

toolLayout.faqSubtitle: Quick answers about usage, privacy, and best practices.
toolLayout.privacyBadgeBody: Everything runs locally in your browser. No data is sent to a server.
toolLayout.privacyNoteBody: This tool runs entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

security.name: Security
security.description: Password generators, hash tools, and security utilities for protecting your data.

dev-utilities.name: Dev Utilities
dev-utilities.description: JSON formatters, encoders, converters, and essential developer tools.

generators.name: Generators
generators.description: Random strings, colors, Lorem Ipsum, and text manipulation tools.

password-generator.title: Password Generator
password-generator.description: Generate cryptographically secure random passwords with customizable length and character sets.
password-generator.seoContent:
## What is a Password Generator?
A password generator creates random, unpredictable passwords using cryptographically secure randomness.

## Why use it?
- Unique passwords per account
- More resistance to brute-force and guessing
- Helps meet password policy requirements

## Privacy
Runs entirely in your browser. Nothing is sent to servers.

faq.a: Yes. It relies on cryptographically secure randomness in the browser.

faq.q: Are my passwords stored or transmitted?
faq.a: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.
Length (16+), randomness, and a mix of character sets.
Yes, once the page is loaded.

password-strength-checker.title: Password Strength Checker
password-strength-checker.description: Analyze password strength with entropy estimation, pattern detection, and improvement suggestions.
password-strength-checker.seoContent:
## What is password strength?
It measures how hard a password is to guess.

## What we check
- Length and character variety
- Common patterns (123, qwerty, repeats)
- Estimated entropy

## Privacy
Your password is analyzed locally and never leaves your device.

faq.q: Is it safe to enter my real password here?
faq.a: Yes. The analysis runs locally in your browser.
A measure of randomness in bits; higher is better.

faq.q: Why does a 'complex-looking' password score low?
faq.a: Because predictable patterns score poorly (e.g. Password123!).

random-token-generator.title: Random Token Generator
random-token-generator.description: Create secure random tokens in hex, base64, or alphanumeric format for sessions, CSRF, and more.
random-token-generator.seoContent:
## What is a random token?
A cryptographically secure string used for sessions, CSRF, reset links, etc.

## Formats
Hex, Base64, URL-safe Base64, and alphanumeric.

## Security
Generated with cryptographically secure randomness in the browser.

faq.q: How many bits of entropy do I need?
faq.a: 128 bits minimum for security tokens; 256 bits for API keys.
Base64 is more compact; hex is simpler and common in databases.

faq.q: Is this suitable for production use?
faq.a: Yes, as it uses cryptographically secure randomness.

api-key-generator.title: API Key Generator
api-key-generator.description: Generate secure API keys with custom prefixes, formats, and lengths for your applications.
api-key-generator.seoContent:
## What is an API key?
A secret token used to authenticate requests to an API.

## Best practices
- Use prefixes (sk_, pk_, live_, test_)
- Rotate periodically
- Store in a secrets manager

## Tip
Store hashed keys in DB when possible.

faq.q: What prefix should I use for API keys?
faq.a: Common: sk_ (secret), pk_ (public) plus live_/test_ for environment.

faq.q: How long should API keys be?
faq.a: At least 32 bytes (~256 bits) for production.

faq.q: Should I hash stored API keys?
faq.a: Yes, store only a hash (e.g., SHA-256) when possible.

bcrypt-hash-generator.title: Bcrypt Hash Generator
bcrypt-hash-generator.description: Generate bcrypt password hashes with adjustable cost factor for secure password storage.
bcrypt-hash-generator.seoContent:
## What is bcrypt?
A slow password hashing function designed to resist brute-force.

## Cost factor
Higher cost = slower hashing = harder to attack.

## Important
In real apps, hash passwords on the server (not client-side).

faq.q: What cost factor should I use?
faq.a: Use the highest that stays under ~500ms on your server. Cost 12 is a common start.

faq.q: Is bcrypt better than SHA-256 for passwords?
faq.a: Yes, because bcrypt is intentionally slow and includes a salt.

faq.q: Can I decrypt a bcrypt hash?
faq.a: No. It's one-way; you only verify by comparing hashes.

jwt-decoder.title: JWT Decoder
jwt-decoder.description: Decode and inspect JSON Web Tokens (JWT) to view header, payload, and signature info.
jwt-decoder.seoContent:
## What is a JWT?
A compact token format for auth: header.payload.signature.

## This tool
Decodes parts for debugging.

## Warning
Decoding is not signature verification; always verify server-side.

faq.q: Does this tool verify the JWT signature?
faq.a: No. It only decodes and displays content.

faq.q: Can someone read my JWT without the secret?
faq.a: Yes. Payload is Base64URL-encoded, not encrypted.
Check the exp claim (Unix time) against current time.

sha256-hash-generator.title: SHA256 Hash Generator
sha256-hash-generator.description: Generate SHA-256 hashes from text using the Web Crypto API. Fast, secure, and runs entirely in your browser.
sha256-hash-generator.seoContent:
## What is SHA-256?
SHA-256 is a cryptographic hash function that produces a 256-bit (64 hex character) fingerprint of any input.

## Use cases
- File integrity verification
- Password hashing (with salt)
- Digital signatures
- Blockchain and cryptocurrencies

## Privacy
Hashing runs 100% in your browser via the Web Crypto API. No data is sent to any server.

faq.a: Yes. It's cryptographically strong and widely used for integrity and signatures.

faq.q: Can I reverse a SHA-256 hash?
faq.a: No. It's a one-way function; you cannot recover the original input.
SHA-256 is more secure. MD5 is broken for security; use SHA-256 for new projects.

md5-hash-generator.title: MD5 Hash Generator
md5-hash-generator.description: Generate MD5 hashes from text. Useful for checksums and legacy compatibility—not recommended for security.
md5-hash-generator.seoContent:
## What is MD5?
MD5 is a 128-bit hash function. It's fast but cryptographically broken.

## When to use
- Legacy systems and APIs
- Non-security checksums (e.g., cache keys)
- File integrity where collision resistance isn't critical

## Security warning
Do not use MD5 for passwords or security-sensitive hashing. Use SHA-256 or bcrypt instead.

faq.a: No. MD5 is broken; use bcrypt or Argon2 for passwords.
Legacy compatibility, cache keys, and non-security checksums.

faq.q: Can two inputs produce the same MD5 hash?
faq.a: Yes. Collisions are known; avoid MD5 for security.

uuid-generator.title: UUID Generator
uuid-generator.description: Generate RFC 4122 compliant UUIDs (v4) using cryptographically secure randomness.
uuid-generator.seoContent:
## What is a UUID?
A 128-bit identifier designed to be globally unique.

## Use cases
DB keys, correlation IDs, distributed systems.

## v4
Random-based UUID, generated with secure randomness.

faq.a: Extremely low in practice; collisions are negligible at real-world scale.

faq.q: Should I use UUID as DB primary key?
faq.a: Depends: great for distributed systems; may affect index performance.
Yes, they follow the v4 spec.

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter & Validator
json-formatter.description: Format, validate, and beautify JSON with error detection.
json-formatter.seoContent:
## JSON formatting
Makes JSON readable and validates syntax.

## Common errors
Trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, comments.

## Tip
Use minify for production payloads; pretty print for debugging.

faq.a: Most common: trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, or comments.

faq.q: Can I add comments to JSON?
faq.a: Standard JSON doesn't support comments. Consider JSONC/JSON5.
Use the Minify option in the tool.

json-minify.title: JSON Minify
json-minify.description: Minify JSON by removing whitespace and newlines. Validates syntax and shows friendly errors.
json-minify.seoContent:
## What is JSON minification?
Minification removes unnecessary whitespace, newlines, and indentation from JSON to reduce size.

## Use cases
- API payloads (smaller requests)
- Config files
- Embedding JSON in HTML/attributes

## Validation
Invalid JSON shows a clear error message. Fix syntax before minifying.

faq.a: Smaller payloads mean faster transfers and lower bandwidth.
No. Minification only removes whitespace; the structure stays the same.

faq.q: My JSON is invalid. What's wrong?
faq.a: Check for trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, or comments.

json-to-csv-converter.title: JSON to CSV Converter
json-to-csv-converter.description: Convert JSON arrays of objects to CSV. Validates JSON, generates headers, and lets you copy or download the result.
json-to-csv-converter.seoContent:
## JSON to CSV
Convert structured JSON into a CSV file for spreadsheets, analytics tools, or quick sharing.

## Best input format
This tool works best with an **array of objects**:

```json
[{"name":"Ada","age":36},{"name":"Linus","age":55}]
```

## Notes
- Headers are derived from object keys
- Missing values become empty cells
- Nested objects/arrays are stringified

## Privacy
Conversion runs locally in your browser. No data is uploaded.

faq.a: The common case is an array of objects. A single object is also supported and will be treated as one row.
Nested objects and arrays are JSON-stringified inside the CSV cell.

faq.q: Why do some values have quotes?
faq.a: CSV requires quoting when values contain commas, quotes, or newlines.

csv-to-json-converter.title: CSV to JSON Converter
csv-to-json-converter.description: Convert CSV to JSON (array of objects). Uses the first row as headers, handles quoted fields, and outputs formatted JSON.
csv-to-json-converter.seoContent:
## CSV to JSON
Convert comma-separated values into JSON for APIs, scripting, and data processing.

## How it works
- First row = headers (property names)
- Each following row becomes an object
- Quoted fields are supported (including escaped quotes)

## Tip
If your CSV has numbers/booleans, enable type inference for a more natural JSON output.

## Privacy
Everything runs in your browser. No server calls.

faq.a: Yes. It supports RFC4180-style quoted fields and escaped quotes ("").

faq.q: What happens if a row has a different number of columns?
faq.a: The tool reports an error so you can fix the input.

faq.q: Does it infer numbers and booleans?
faq.a: Optionally. When enabled, values like 42, true, false, and null are converted to proper JSON types.

json-diff-compare.title: JSON Diff / JSON Compare
json-diff-compare.description: Compare two JSON documents to see if they are equivalent and get a readable list of differences by path.
json-diff-compare.seoContent:
## JSON compare
Paste **JSON A** and **JSON B** to check whether they represent the same data.

## What you get
- Validates both JSON inputs
- Reports whether they are equivalent
- Lists differences with paths like `$..key` and `$.items[0]`

## Notes
- Object key order does not matter
- Array order does matter

## Privacy
Comparison runs locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.

faq.a: No. Objects are compared by keys/values regardless of ordering.
Yes. Arrays are compared by index, so reordering items will produce differences.
No. It shows a readable diff list; it does not output JSON Patch/RFC6902.

base64-encoder-decoder.title: Base64 Encoder/Decoder
base64-encoder-decoder.description: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings back to plain text.
base64-encoder-decoder.seoContent:
## Base64
Binary-to-text encoding used in APIs, emails, JWT, data URLs.

## Not encryption
Anyone can decode Base64. Don't use it to hide secrets.

faq.a: No. It's encoding, not encryption.
It adds ~33% overhead due to encoding.

faq.q: When should I use URL-safe Base64?
faq.a: When the value will appear in URLs.

url-encoder-decoder.title: URL Encoder/Decoder
url-encoder-decoder.description: Encode special characters for URLs or decode percent-encoded strings.
url-encoder-decoder.seoContent:
## URL encoding
Converts unsafe characters to %XX sequences.

## Use cases
Query params, form submissions, APIs, i18n URLs.

## Tip
Use encodeURIComponent for individual values.

faq.a: Encode user input before adding it to a URL.
+ is common in query strings; %20 is standard encoding.

faq.q: Encode entire URL or a param?
faq.a: encodeURI for full URL, encodeURIComponent for params.

html-encoder-decoder.title: HTML Encoder/Decoder
html-encoder-decoder.description: Encode text to HTML entities or decode HTML entities back to plain text. Handles < > & " and more.
html-encoder-decoder.seoContent:
## HTML encoding
Converts special characters to HTML entities so they display safely in web pages.

## Entities
< > & " ' — prevents script injection and displays reserved chars.

## Use cases
User-generated content, escaping output, preventing XSS.

## Not encryption
Anyone can decode. Use for display safety, not secrecy.

faq.a: When displaying user input or untrusted content in HTML to prevent XSS.
HTML encode for body content; URL encode for query params and URLs.
It encodes the main five; decoding handles common numeric and named entities.

timestamp-converter.title: Timestamp Converter
timestamp-converter.description: Convert between Unix timestamps, ISO 8601, and human-readable formats.
timestamp-converter.seoContent:
## Unix timestamp
Seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC (epoch).

## Beware
JavaScript uses milliseconds (13 digits) vs seconds (10 digits).

faq.a: 10 digits usually seconds; 13 digits usually milliseconds.
Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) for seconds.

faq.q: Why does my DB show different values?
faq.a: Different DBs store time with different precisions and timezones.

regex-tester.title: Regex Tester
regex-tester.description: Test and debug regular expressions with real-time matching and groups.
regex-tester.seoContent:
## Regex
Patterns for matching text.

## Useful for
Validation, parsing, search/replace.

## Tip
Remember to escape special chars and choose proper flags (g,i,m).

faq.a: Check escaping, anchors (^ $), and flags.

faq.q: What does the g flag do?
faq.a: Finds all matches instead of stopping at the first.
Use the multiline (m) flag and/or dotAll depending on engine.

random-string-generator.title: Random String Generator
random-string-generator.description: Generate random strings with custom character sets, lengths, and formats.
random-string-generator.seoContent:
## Random strings
Useful for placeholders, testing, identifiers.

## Options
Upper/lowercase, numbers, symbols, custom charset.

faq.a: They use cryptographically secure randomness in the browser.
This tool generates random strings; patterns require a different approach.

lorem-ipsum-generator.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator
lorem-ipsum-generator.description: Generate placeholder text as paragraphs, sentences, or words.
lorem-ipsum-generator.seoContent:
## Lorem Ipsum
Classic placeholder text used in design and development.

## Use
Mock layouts without distracting real content.

faq.a: It comes from scrambled Latin (Cicero), used as filler text.

faq.q: Should I use Lorem Ipsum in production?
faq.a: No, it's for mockups and development.

random-number-generator.title: Random Number Generator
random-number-generator.description: Generate random numbers within a range (integers or decimals).
random-number-generator.seoContent:
## Random numbers
Generate values for tests, games, and simulations.

## Options
Min/max, integer/decimal, batch generation.

faq.a: Generated with cryptographically secure randomness in the browser.
Yes, enable unique mode when generating multiple integers.

random-color-generator.title: Random Color Generator
random-color-generator.description: Generate random colors in HEX, RGB, and HSL with preview and palette options.
random-color-generator.seoContent:
## Random colors
Useful for UI experiments and design inspiration.

## Formats
HEX, RGB, HSL and quick preview.

faq.a: Use harmony rules (complementary/analogous) if available.

faq.q: Can I limit the color range?
faq.a: Yes, constrain hue/saturation/lightness if the tool supports it.

slugify-text.title: Slugify Text
slugify-text.description: Convert text to URL-friendly slugs with separators and transliteration.
slugify-text.seoContent:
## Slug
A URL-friendly version of text.

Example
"My Blog Post!" → "my-blog-post".

## Tip
Hyphens are preferred for SEO.

faq.a: Hyphens are preferred; search engines treat them as word separators.
Keep them concise (often under ~60 characters).

text-case-converter.title: Text Case Converter
text-case-converter.description: Convert text between lowercase, UPPERCASE, Title Case, camelCase, snake_case and more.
text-case-converter.seoContent:
## Case conversion
Quickly transform text for programming and writing.

Includes
camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, kebab-case, CONSTANT_CASE.

faq.a: camelCase starts lowercase; PascalCase starts uppercase.
snake_case common in code/DB; kebab-case common in URLs/CSS.

whitespace-cleaner.title: Whitespace Cleaner
whitespace-cleaner.description: Remove extra whitespace, trim lines, normalize line endings, and clean messy text.
whitespace-cleaner.seoContent:
## Whitespace cleaning
Fix messy text by trimming, collapsing spaces, and normalizing line endings.

Helpful for
Copy/paste from PDFs, emails, logs.

faq.a: Windows uses CRLF; Linux/macOS use LF.
Zero-width spaces or special whitespace that can break comparisons.

markdown-preview.title: Markdown Preview
markdown-preview.description: Write and preview Markdown with real-time rendering.
markdown-preview.seoContent:
## Markdown preview
Write Markdown and see the rendered output instantly.

Great for
READMEs, documentation, notes.

faq.a: GitHub-flavored Markdown (GFM) is the usual standard.
If the tool offers it, use Copy HTML / Export options.

qr-code-generator.title: QR Code Generator
qr-code-generator.description: Generate QR codes for URLs, text, Wi‑Fi, and more. Preview instantly and download as PNG or SVG.
qr-code-generator.seoContent:
## What is a QR code generator?
A QR code generator creates a scannable QR image that encodes data such as a URL, text, or configuration payload.

## Common use cases
- Share a URL quickly
- Print QR codes for menus, posters, events
- Encode short text snippets
- Wi‑Fi setup codes (when using the proper format)

## Privacy
QR generation runs locally in your browser. Your input is not uploaded.

## Tips
- Use higher error correction when printing or when the QR might be damaged
- Keep payloads short for simpler, easier-to-scan codes

faq.q: Is my data sent to a server?
faq.a: No. QR generation happens locally in your browser.

faq.q: PNG or SVG—which should I download?
faq.a: SVG is best for printing and scaling. PNG is convenient for sharing and images.
QR codes can include redundancy so scanners can recover data even if parts are missing or damaged.

ui.placeholder: Paste a URL or type text to generate a QR code...
ui.emptyBody: Enter text or a URL above to generate a QR code preview.
ui.privacyBody: This tool runs entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

word-counter.title: Word Counter
word-counter.description: Count words, lines, paragraphs, and characters in your text. Simple and fast.
word-counter.seoContent:
## Word counter
Count words, lines, paragraphs, and characters in real time.

## Use cases
- Blog posts and articles
- Essays and assignments
- SEO meta descriptions
- Social media limits

## How it works
Words are split by whitespace. Paragraphs are blocks separated by blank lines.

faq.a: Words are split by whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines).
Blocks of text separated by one or more blank lines.
Yes. Everything runs in your browser; no data is sent.

character-counter.title: Character Counter
character-counter.description: Count characters with and without spaces. Useful for tweets, meta descriptions, and character limits.
character-counter.seoContent:
## Character counter
Count total characters and characters without spaces.

## Use cases
- Twitter/X (280 chars)
- Meta descriptions (~155)
- SMS limits
- Form validation

## Stats
Shows total chars, chars without spaces, plus words and lines for context.

faq.a: With spaces = full length. Without spaces = excludes spaces, tabs, newlines.
Twitter counts differently (e.g. URLs). This is a simple character count.
Yes. Everything runs locally in your browser.
